packets.description: Questions related to self-awareness and emotional self-knowledge

scoringScale.label: Development priority
scoringScale.largeText: Developing self-awareness should be a key focus area for you right now. You may not always pause to reflect on your emotions, behavior, or the effect your actions have on others but learning to do so can make a powerful difference in how you think, respond, and grow. Self-awareness is the foundation of emotional intelligence, and strengthening it will help you make better choices, manage emotions effectively, and build more genuine relationships. Start by creating moments of reflection in your day: notice what triggers your reactions, what gives you energy, and what drains it. Writing down your thoughts, seeking honest feedback from trusted people, or simply asking yourself “Why did I react that way?” can help you see patterns more clearly. As you work on this area, you’ll begin to understand yourself with greater clarity and confidence. This will help you stay grounded under pressure, communicate more thoughtfully, and act with purpose rather than impulse. Making self-awareness a development priority will not only improve your emotional strength but also enhance your leadership, relationships, and overall sense of fulfilment.

scoringScale.label: Needs attention
scoringScale.largeText: You may find it difficult to clearly recognize your emotions, thoughts, and the impact they have on your behavior or relationships. This is not uncommon, but it’s an important area that can benefit from greater focus and reflection. Building self-awareness will help you make better decisions, communicate more effectively, and manage your emotions in a healthier way. Start by paying closer attention to how you react in different situations that triggers stress, irritation, or confidence in you. Writing your thoughts or reflecting for a few minutes each day can help you notice patterns in your behavior and emotional responses. Seeking honest feedback from people you trust can also give valuable insight into how others perceive you. As you develop stronger self-awareness, you’ll begin to understand your emotions before they take over, and you’ll respond more thoughtfully in challenging moments. Over time, this will make you more confident, emotionally balanced, and effective in how you connect and lead both at work and in your personal life.

scoringScale.label: Strength
scoringScale.largeText: You have a healthy level of self-awareness, which means you understand your strengths, limitations, emotions, and the impact you have on others. This awareness helps you make thoughtful decisions and handle situations with maturity. Because you are able to recognize your emotional patterns, you can manage your reactions and maintain balance even in challenging moments. Your ability to reflect on your thoughts and behaviors allows you to learn from experiences rather than repeat mistakes. You have a realistic view of yourself confident yet grounded which helps you adapt well to different environments, whether personal or professional. To keep this strength growing, continue practicing honest self-reflection and welcome feedback from others. This will help you stay aware of how you come across and where you can improve. Your balanced self-awareness not only supports personal growth but also builds authenticity, trust, and emotional stability, the qualities that naturally inspire confidence in those around you.

scoringScale.label: Excellent
scoringScale.largeText: Outstanding! You've mastered this material!

packets.name: Emotional Regulation
packets.description: Questions related to emotional regulation and management

scoringScale.label: Development priority
scoringScale.largeText: Managing emotions can be particularly challenging right now, and this area requires focused and immediate attention. You may find that your feelings sometimes take the lead, influencing your tone, decisions, or reactions more than you intend. Making emotional regulation a key development goal will help you regain balance, build resilience, and strengthen your overall emotional intelligence. Start by increasing emotional awareness. Notice early signs of stress or frustration and practice pausing before reacting. Deep breathing, short reflection breaks, or grounding exercises can help calm your mind and bring clarity in tense moments. Over time, this will help you shift from reacting impulsively to responding thoughtfully. Developing this skill will make a visible difference, you’ll be able to think more clearly under pressure, manage conflicts with maturity, and maintain a steady sense of control even when emotions run high. This focused work on emotional balance will not only improve your relationships but also shape you into a calmer, more composed, and emotionally strong individual capable of leading yourself and others effectively.

scoringScale.label: Needs attention
scoringScale.largeText: At times, you may find it challenging to manage your emotions, especially in stressful or high-pressure situations. This doesn’t mean you lack emotional strength, it simply means this is an area where conscious effort can bring meaningful growth. Recognizing how your emotions influence your reactions and decisions is the first step toward building better control and balance. Start by pausing before responding when emotions run high. Try to identify what you’re feeling and why, this simple awareness can prevent impulsive reactions and help you respond more thoughtfully. Practicing calm breathing, journaling, or talking things out with someone you trust can also help you process emotions in a healthy way. As you learn to manage emotions more effectively, you’ll notice a positive shift, your confidence will grow, your communication will become clearer, and your relationships will feel smoother and more respectful. Over time, developing this skill will help you handle challenges with greater ease and bring more emotional stability and peace into your life.

scoringScale.label: Strength
scoringScale.largeText: You have a good sense of control over your emotions, which helps you stay calm, composed, and clear-headed even when situations get stressful. This balance allows you to respond thoughtfully instead of reacting impulsively, which is a valuable strength in both personal and professional life. Your ability to recognize what you’re feeling and manage those feelings in a healthy way helps you maintain steady relationships and handle pressure with maturity. People around you likely see you as dependable and emotionally grounded because you don’t let stress or frustration take charge of your actions. This emotional steadiness not only supports your own well-being but also sets a positive tone for others. Whether it’s managing disagreements, making important decisions, or supporting others in tough times, your balanced emotional control allows you to bring stability, confidence, and a sense of calm wherever you go.

scoringScale.label: Excellent
scoringScale.largeText: Outstanding! You've mastered this material!

packets.name: Self Drive
packets.description: Questions related to self-motivation and drive

scoringScale.label: Development priority
scoringScale.largeText: Right now, staying motivated may feel like a challenge, and this is an area that needs focused and consistent attention. Self-motivation is the inner energy that pushes you to act even when things get difficult or results are not immediate. Making it a development priority will help you strengthen your confidence, discipline, and ability to stay committed to your goals. Begin by identifying what truly inspires you, your personal reasons for doing what you do. Setting clear, meaningful goals and breaking them into smaller, achievable steps can help you build momentum. Each small success fuels your inner drive and keeps you moving forward. Creating positive daily habits also plays a big role so start your day with intention, maintain a routine, and remind yourself of how far you’ve come. Over time, as you learn to rely on your own determination rather than external push, you’ll notice a major shift. You’ll become more resilient, focused, and confident in pursuing what matters most to you. Developing this quality will not only enhance your performance but also bring a deeper sense of purpose and satisfaction to your life.

scoringScale.label: Needs attention
scoringScale.largeText: At times, you may find it difficult to stay consistent or push yourself when challenges arise. This does not mean you lack ability however it simply shows that your internal drive needs a bit more focus and direction. Building self-motivation starts with finding why a goal matters to you and using that sense of purpose to keep going, even when things get tough. Begin by setting small, realistic goals that give you a sense of progress and accomplishment. Celebrate small wins and remind yourself of the bigger picture you are working toward. Surrounding yourself with encouraging people and keeping a positive routine can also help you stay on track. As you strengthen your inner motivation, you’ll notice greater confidence, consistency, and satisfaction in your efforts. Over time, this focus will help you move from simply completing tasks to truly enjoying your journey of growth and success.

scoringScale.label: Strength
scoringScale.largeText: You have a healthy and steady level of self-motivation, which helps you stay focused and committed to your goals. Even when situations become challenging, you are able to push yourself forward and maintain a positive attitude. This inner drive allows you to take initiative, complete tasks on time, and stay productive without needing constant external reminders. Your balanced motivation means you set realistic goals and follow them through with consistency rather than rushing or giving up midway. You are likely to find satisfaction in your progress and keep improving yourself, which is a great quality for long-term growth. To keep this strength alive, continue setting small, meaningful goals and celebrate every achievement, no matter how small. Surround yourself with people and environments that inspire you. Your self-driven nature not only keeps you moving ahead but also encourages others around you to stay determined and focused on their own growth.

scoringScale.label: Excellent
scoringScale.largeText: Outstanding! You've mastered this material!

packets.name: Understanding Others
packets.description: Questions related to understanding and sharing others' emotions

scoringScale.label: Development priority
scoringScale.largeText: Empathy is the ability to understand and connect with what others are feeling, helping you respond with warmth and sensitivity. Strengthening this area will allow you to build deeper, more trusting relationships and communicate with greater ease. Sometimes, in the rush of daily life, it’s easy to focus on tasks and miss the emotions behind people’s words. Making a conscious effort to slow down, listen attentively, and notice how others feel can make a big difference. Even small acts like showing patience, offering reassurance, or simply being present can help people feel valued and supported. As you continue to develop empathy, you’ll find your interactions becoming more positive and fulfilling. It will enhance your ability to collaborate, lead with understanding, and create a sense of comfort and trust around you. With consistent effort, empathy can become one of your most natural and admired strengths.

scoringScale.label: Needs attention
scoringScale.largeText: Empathy is the ability to understand and connect with what others are feeling, helping you respond with warmth and sensitivity. At times, you may find it difficult to fully understand or relate to what others are feeling, which can make your interactions seem distant or misunderstood. This does not mean you lack care however, it simply means that your focus or patience in understanding others’ emotions may need more attention right now. Developing empathy starts with small steps: listening without judgment, observing others’ tone and body language, and trying to see situations from their point of view. As you practice these, you’ll begin to sense emotions more naturally and respond with greater warmth and understanding. Improving your empathy will help you build stronger, more trusting relationships at work and in your personal life. It will also enhance your emotional intelligence, allowing you to communicate more effectively, handle conflicts with maturity, and create deeper, more meaningful connections with people around you.

scoringScale.label: Strength
scoringScale.largeText: You have a natural ability to sense what others are going through and respond with kindness and understanding. You listen not just to words but also to emotions, which helps people feel heard and respected. Your empathy is balanced which means you can connect deeply with others without getting emotionally overwhelmed yourself. In your relationships, both at work and in your personal life, people are likely to feel comfortable opening up to you because you make them feel valued and supported. This quality helps you handle conflicts gently, build trust easily, and create a positive environment wherever you go. Your ability to understand people’s feelings while keeping healthy emotional boundaries makes you a strong team member, an effective communicator, and someone others naturally turn to for guidance and comfort.

scoringScale.label: Excellent
scoringScale.largeText: Outstanding! You've mastered this material!

packets.name: Interpersonal Effectiveness
packets.description: Questions related to social interaction and relationship management

scoringScale.label: Development priority
scoringScale.largeText: Strengthening your social skills should be a key focus area for you right now. You may sometimes find it difficult to start conversations, express ideas clearly, or build rapport in group situations. Making this a development priority will help you communicate more confidently, connect better with others, and build relationships that support both your personal and professional growth. Begin by practicing open and positive communication, make an effort to listen actively, maintain eye contact, and show genuine interest in what others are saying. Simple gestures like offering appreciation, asking questions, or remembering small details about people can go a long way in building trust and warmth. Working on your social interactions will not only make you more approachable but will also enhance teamwork, networking, and leadership abilities. As you become more comfortable engaging with others, you’ll notice stronger connections, smoother collaborations, and a greater sense of belonging. With consistent effort, this development will significantly boost your confidence and overall effectiveness in all areas of life.

scoringScale.label: Needs attention
scoringScale.largeText: Your social skills may need some conscious effort and development. You might occasionally find it challenging to communicate openly, build quick rapport, or navigate group dynamics with ease. Paying attention to this area can make a noticeable difference in how you connect with others and how effectively you collaborate at work or in personal settings. Improving social skills begins with simple actions, maintaining eye contact, actively listening, showing appreciation, and responding with empathy. Engaging in open and respectful communication helps you express your ideas more clearly and understand others’ perspectives better. As you focus on strengthening this skill, you’ll find yourself becoming more confident in conversations, more adaptable in group situations, and better at resolving conflicts. Over time, this will help you build stronger relationships, earn trust, and create a more positive and cooperative atmosphere around you.

scoringScale.label: Strength
scoringScale.largeText: You have a well-developed ability to interact and connect with people, which helps you build strong and positive relationships. Your communication style is approachable and respectful, allowing others to feel comfortable sharing their thoughts and ideas with you. Whether in teamwork, discussions, or daily interactions, you can express yourself clearly and listen to others with attention and understanding. Your balanced social skills also help you handle differences of opinion in a calm and constructive way. You know how to cooperate with others, find common ground, and maintain harmony even in challenging situations. This ability not only supports collaboration but also makes you someone people trust and enjoy working with. To keep strengthening this quality, continue practicing open communication, empathy, and active listening. These small habits will help you build deeper connections and make your interactions even more effective. Your natural ease with people is a true strength that will continue to benefit your personal growth and professional success.

scoringScale.label: Excellent
scoringScale.largeText: Outstanding! You've mastered this material!

quizzes.name: Emotional Intelligence Assessment
quizzes.description: Comprehensive assessment covering all 5 emotional intelligence areas

quizzes.name: HappiLife
quizzes.report_header:
# **Mental Wellness**

Mental Wellness constitutes of:

- **Behavioural Health:** How we behave  
- **Cognitive Health:** How we think  
- **Emotional Health:** How we feel  
- **Quality of Life:** Facets of physical health, social relations, spirituality, etc.  

---

## **Summary Overview**

The summary provides you with a **snapshot of your mental wellbeing, virtues, and vices**.  

The first step towards emotional wellness is **awareness of your capacities** — the beginning point of your wellness journey.  

This summary:  
- Compiles your strengths, opportunities, and various facets of your personality  
- Scales your emotional wellness across **10 parameters** based on your screening responses  
- Acts as a **mirror**, describing and guiding you towards holistic wellness  

---

## **Next Step**

You can also opt for an **assisted summary reading** with a qualified mental wellness expert to gain deeper insights and actionable steps.  

---

## **Disclaimer**

⚠️ The summary is **indicative** and **not equivalent to medical advice**.
quizzes.report_footer:
# **Next Step**

Congratulations on completing your **HappiLIFE Awareness tool** and going through the entire summary. Now that you know how the **HappiMynd wheel** works, we are sure you have gained a comprehensive understanding of your emotional, behavioral, and cognitive wellbeing.  

You are now well equipped to identify the areas of life that you are thriving in and the ones that need to be worked upon.  

If you are keen on making the most out of your summary, an **assisted Summary Reading session** by our emotional wellbeing expert will guide you in:  
- Minutely scrutinizing and interpreting your performance under each parameter  
- Understanding what implications the scores carry  
- Guiding you on the necessary next steps that can set you sailing on a holistic wellness journey  

Once aware of your needs, you can choose from our **unique range of accessible, affordable & reliable services** available over a fully digital, human-assisted platform while ensuring utmost confidentiality.

---

## **Recommendations**

| **Score (in any parameter)** | **Recommended Tool** |
|-------------------------------|-----------------------|
| > 5                           | HappiBUDDY, HappiLEARN |
| 3 – 5                         | HappiGUIDE, HappiSELF |
| < 3                           | HappiTALK             |

---

## **Our Services**

- **HappiGUIDE**  
  Helps you to make the most out of your HappiLIFE summary with a summary reading session by our emotional wellbeing expert.  

- **HappiLEARN**  
  An online self-help library that enriches you with 24×7 access to 5000+ minutes of curated, well-researched content including videos, audios, blogs, and more.  

- **HappiBUDDY**  
  Allows you to connect with a professional expert buddy in a personal emotional log room that is non-judgemental, anonymous, and 100% confidential.  

- **HappiSELF**  
  A mobile application that enables **self-management** of emotional wellbeing with a globally validated, interactive program based on Cognitive Behavior Therapy (CBT).  

- **HappiTALK**  
  Offers you a safe space to discuss life, aspirations, personal issues, relationships, and more with the best of our country’s experts — all from the comfort of your home.  

---

## **Contact Details**

📧 Email: [info@happimynd.com](mailto:info@happimynd.com)  
📞 Phone: 9110599581  
🌐 Website: [www.happimynd.com](http://www.happimynd.com)  

---

## **Disclaimer**

**A. For users affiliated with a company/organization enrolled with HappiMynd services:**  
1. The user can avail only those services which the affiliated company has subscribed/purchased.  
2. If the user wishes to avail services not covered by the company, they may make an individual/personal purchase.  
3. The available services and their prices for individual users can be found on the HappiMynd app or website dashboard.  

**B. Important Note:**  
- This summary supports self-discovery, identifying areas of improvement, and living a holistic life.  
- It is **indicative** and **not a replacement for medical advice**.  
- The statements in HappiLIFE Awareness tool are inspired by ICD-10 (WHO) & DSM-5® guidelines.  

---

## **Helpline Numbers**

- National Emergency: **112**  
- Women Helpline: **1091**  
- Senior Citizen Helpline: **14567**  
- Suicide Prevention (AASRA): **9820466726**

quizzes.name: HappiEQ
quizzes.report_header:
# HappiEQ Self Assessment

## About this Summary

Emotional Intelligence (measured as Emotional Quotient – EQ) is at the heart of how we understand ourselves and relate to others. It influences the way we manage emotional balance, communicate, make decisions and build meaningful relationships.

This summary provides you with a snapshot of your EQ profile across key dimensions—**self-image, emotional-regulation, self drive, understanding others, and interpersonal effectiveness.**

The purpose of this report is to highlight your emotional strengths and growth areas, giving you a clearer picture of how your emotional intelligence shows up in daily life. By becoming aware of these patterns, you take the first step towards enhancing your personal effectiveness, building stronger connections, and thriving both personally and professionally.
quizzes.report_footer:
## Next Step

Congratulations on completing your HappiEQ assessment and exploring your summary. Now that you have insights into your emotional strengths and areas for improvement, you are well-prepared to take practical steps toward growth. Each score and reflection in this report points you to actionable opportunities whether it’s improving self-regulation under pressure, deepening empathy or strengthening communication.

To make the most of your EQ journey, we recommend engaging in guided reflection sessions, structured learning modules and practical exercises tailored to your results. These will help you translate awareness into everyday habits and long-term resilience. Professional support is available on the HappiMynd app. 
 
Turn awareness into action—speak with an expert to unlock insights and chart your way forward.  
Remember, emotional intelligence is not fixed; it can be nurtured and strengthened with consistent practice. This report is your roadmap to build long-lasting emotional agility.

### Score Table

| Score  | Level               | Description                                                                                                                                         | Recommendation                                                                                                                           | Tools                        |
|-------:|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| 35–50  | Balanced            | Keep building depth by practicing advanced skills such as conflict resolution, mentoring others, or leading with empathy.                            | Engage in peer mentoring or leadership circles to refine your skills. Practice mindfulness and reflection to maintain balance under pressure.| HappiSELF & HappiLEARN       |
| 18–34  | Needs Attention     | You have a good foundation but there’s room to strengthen certain areas. Focus on building awareness, practicing regulation techniques.               | Focus on deepening emotional regulation under stress. Practice empathetic listening by summarizing and reflecting back what others share. Use journaling or feedback tools to identify blind spots in self-awareness. | HappiSELF & HappiBUDDY       |
| 10–17  | Development Priority| This is a growth area for you. Developing EQ will bring visible changes in how you connect with yourself and others. Personalized guidance can help. | Work with a coach or psychologist to develop core EQ skills. Start with guided self-reflection exercises to identify emotional triggers. Practice empathy-building activities, such as perspective-taking in daily interactions. | HappiTALK & HappiBUDDY       |

### Support Services (via HappiMynd App)

- **HappiGUIDE** – Get expert-led insights on your profile and create a clear roadmap for growth. Together, you'll discover what your results truly mean and create a practical action plan tailored just for you.

- **HappiLEARN** – *Your emotional wellbeing library—open 24/7.* With HappiLEARN, you get unlimited access to 5000+ minutes of curated videos, audios, blogs, and tools designed by experts to practice empathy, regulation, and resilience at your own pace.

- **HappiBUDDY** – Confidential space to enhance your relational EQ *because everyone needs someone to talk to or just a safe space to vent out our emotions.* HappiBUDDY connects you with a trusted professional “buddy” in a safe, private, and judgment-free space so you never have to face challenges alone.

- **HappiSELF** – Empowers you to manage your emotional health with interactive programs based on Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT). Build habits, track progress, and grow stronger every day.

- **HappiTALK** – A safe space for real conversations, allowing you to have meaningful discussions with experts to improve communication, relationships, and emotional expression.

---

### Contact Details
For further details you may contact us at **info@happimynd.com** or [WhatsApp Chat](https://wa.me/919136899581?text=EQ) or download our mobile app:  

[![Download on Google Play](https://happimynd.com/assets/Frontend/images/play_store.png)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.happimynd)
[![Download on App Store](https://happimynd.com/assets/Frontend/images/app_store.png)](https://apps.apple.com/in/app/happimynd-emotional-self-help/id1634742782)

---

### Disclaimer
A. If the services are availed by a person who belongs/works with a company/organization which are enrolled with the services for its employees or has a tie-up with HappiMynd, the services/tools available to the users are subject to the following terms:

1. The user can avail only those services which the affiliated company has subscribed/purchased for its employees.  
2. If the user is willing to avail services which are not covered/subscribed/purchased by the affiliated company, then the user can make an individual/personal purchase of the required services.  
3. The services available and their prices for an individual user can be found on the dashboard of the HappiMynd app or website itself.

B. This summary can support you in discovering yourself, knowing the areas of improvement and living a holistic life. However, it is indicative and not a replacement for any medical advice. The statements used in HappiEQ are inspired by the work of EQ experts across the globe.

If you are having difficult thoughts or going through rough times, please consider calling the helpline numbers below:

- **National Emergency No.** – 112  
- **Women Helpline** – 1091  
- **Senior Citizen Helpline** – 14567  
- **Suicide Prevention (AASRA)** – 9820466726
